items.title: Common distributions (PMF/PDF building blocks)
items.description: Every likelihood factor is a Bernoulli, Binomial, Poisson, Categorical, or Gaussian PMF/PDF evaluated at observed data with parameters θ.

items.title: Maximum likelihood estimation
items.description: MLE chooses θ̂ = argmax_θ L(θ). Optimizing log-likelihood (or negative log-likelihood as loss) is the computational heart of MLE.

items.title: Bayes' theorem
items.description: Posterior ∝ prior × likelihood: P(θ | data) ∝ P(θ) · L(θ). The same L(θ) from this topic is the likelihood in Bayes' rule.

items.title: Gaussian Naive Bayes
items.description: GNB scores each class by prior × product of Gaussian likelihoods f(x_j | μ_kj, σ_kj) over features; each factor is L(θ) for that feature's parameters.
